summarization.a: The line chart shows the mass change over time at 250°C. TMA exposure produces net mass loss, while HF produces net mass gain. The bracketed MCPC indicates the smallest net mass change per cycle on a panel.
summarization.b: The line chart displays the mass change over time at 275°C. TMA exposure produces net mass loss, while HF produces net mass gain.
summarization.c: The line chart illustrates the mass change over time at 325°C. TMA exposure produces net mass loss, while HF produces net mass gain. The bracketed MCPC indicates the largest net mass change per cycle on a panel.

c.question: Which half-cycle dominates the net MCPC at 325 °C ?

a.question: How many ALE cycles are shown on the chart?
Does the cycle result in net etching or net deposition?

b.question: Between the first and second ALE cycles shown on the figure, which cycle shows a larger magnitude MCPC?
b.answer: First cycle (−11 ng/cm²) shows larger MCPC that the second cycle (−9 ng/cm²).
